title: The Psychology of Money
subtitle: Timeless Lessons on Wealth, Greed, and Happiness
author: Morgan Housel
themes: Financial success is driven by behavior, not intelligence
The role of luck and risk in financial outcomes
Compounding and the power of time
The difference between being rich and being wealthy
Freedom and control over time as the highest dividend of money
excerpt: Doing well with money is not about how smart you are — it is about how you behave, and behavior is far harder to teach than formulas or spreadsheets.
rationale: Unconventional addition: includes at least 2-3 unconventional picks per 10-book leaf. The Psychology of Money occupies a unique position as the rare finance besteller that rejects formulaic personal finance advice, emphasizes 'reasonable > rational,' and explicitly challenges the technical-investing orthodoxy by arguing that emotional self-awareness matters more than market knowledge.
